ctxMarket: Attention is focused on institutional adoption and infrastructure developments for Solana. Several traditional finance entities and major banks are expanding their blockchain strategies to include Solana, while social activity around the network has declined.
ctxTalking:
• MoneyGram is now running a Solana validator, signaling further integration of blockchain payments.
• Baillie Gifford and BNY Mellon launched a tokenized fund on Solana and Ethereum, targeting institutional investors.
• Kraken added 2,500 unapproved Solana tokens to its app, emphasizing user responsibility for on-chain risk.
ctxMatters: Institutional involvement may increase Solana’s credibility and usage in mainstream finance. New products like tokenized funds and expanded exchange offerings highlight growing demand for Solana-based assets. Declining social activity contrasts with rising liquidity, raising questions about user engagement versus capital flows.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Major financial institutions and banks are actively building and testing on Solana.
Noise: Short-term fluctuations in social media activity without clear impact on network fundamentals.
ctxGlossary:
Validator — A node that helps secure and verify transactions on a blockchain.
Tokenized fund — An investment fund represented by blockchain tokens, allowing digital ownership and transfer.
TVL (Total Value Locked) — The total value of assets deposited in a blockchain protocol.

readMore.title: MoneyGram becomes Solana validator, expands blockchain payments strategy
TradFi fund manager Baillie Gifford introduces Solana, Ethereum tokenized fund with BNY
Kraken adds 2,500 unapproved Solana tokens to its app – says risk stays on-chain
